regulatory_context.value: Article 4 AI literacy duties already apply from 2 February 2025 to providers and deployers of AI systems, while the CSSF/BCL thematic review shows why AI governance, explainability, ethics, bias, fairness, and auditability matter in Luxembourg finance. The reviewed EU guidance does not impose a finance-specific template or governance board.

training_support_route.value: Luxembourg company-training co-funding covers external training providers and offers 15% support, with a 35% wage-cost uplift for qualifying participants.

value: Product-specific Fit 4 AI applicability is unverified
No public CSSF, CNPD, or accreditation route was found for A4 itself
The 461+ Luxembourg financial-institutions count is unverified
The 20-30% operational cost-reduction claim is unverified
AML/KYC automation and audit-documentation deliverables remain product-design claims

target_audience_summary.value: Luxembourg banks, funds, PSFs, insurers, and adjacent compliance, risk, and operations teams using or evaluating AI.
target_audience_summary.omission_reason: Useful positioning context, but still partly inferential and not a default dashboard fact.

possible_ai_support_route.value: Fit 4 AI is a real Luxembourg SME assessment-and-roadmap route with co-financing, but the reviewed page does not prove that A4 itself is eligible or that any 30% training rate applies.
possible_ai_support_route.omission_reason: The public route exists, but product-level fit remains unverified.
